reason: cross-spawn@7.0.3 — flagged from /opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/jupyterlab/tests/mock_packages/test_no_hyphens/yarn.lock. This is a JupyterLab test fixture lockfile (used by the upstream JupyterLab test suite to exercise extension-build behaviour) and is not consumed by any runtime path in the container; the python3 Jupyter kernel is removed at build time (Dockerfile.{cuda,cpu} `rm -rf /usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels/python3/kernel.json`), so there is no notebook entry point. The yarn.lock is a static fixture, never resolved or executed. Latest jupyterlab 4.5.7 still ships these fixtures with the same pin; no upstream fix is available without patching jupyterlab's own test data.

curl/libcurl@8.11.0 — flagged from /opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/tensorflow/include/external/curl/include/curl/curlver.h. This is a header-only curl version stamp shipped inside the TensorFlow wheel for downstream C++-extension builds; no compiled libcurl binary or shared library is bundled. The OS libcurl (which is what runs at execution time) is refreshed at build time via `dnf upgrade -y libcurl libcurl-minimal --refresh` in Dockerfile.{cuda,cpu}. Will be cleared once the TensorFlow upstream rolls its bundled curl version (tracked in tensorflow/third_party/curl).

cuda-toolkit-config-common@12.9.79 ships with the nvidia/cuda:12.9.1-base-amzn2023 base image. Advisory affects the NVIDIA driver / privileged GPU tooling reachable only by a local attacker on the host; the container itself does not invoke the affected codepath at training time. Will be picked up via the next CUDA base image refresh from NVIDIA.

cuda-toolkit-config-common@12.9.79 ships with the nvidia/cuda:12.9.1-base-amzn2023 base image. Advisory affects an NVML/driver path reachable only by a local privileged caller on the host; not on the in-container training/inference path. Will be picked up via the next CUDA base image refresh from NVIDIA.

tmp@0.0.33 vendored in jupyterlab/staging/yarn.lock (build-tooling lockfile shipped inside the jupyterlab 4.5.7 wheel). The staging/ tree is only used by `jupyter lab build` to rebuild the JupyterLab app from extensions; it is not on the notebook-serve, training, or inference path. JupyterLab is pulled in transitively via sparkmagic and the python3 kernel is removed in the DLC, so there is no notebook entrypoint at runtime. Path-traversal also requires attacker-controlled prefix/postfix/dir flowing into tmp, which does not happen in a static yarn build. Latest jupyterlab 4.5.7 (and main) still pin tmp@^0.0.33, so no upstream fix is available without patching the yarn lockfile upstream.
